concepts.name: Inflation
concepts.definition: The rate at which the general level of prices for goods and services rises, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of money.

concepts.name: Consumer Price Index (CPI)
concepts.definition: A measure of inflation that tracks the price changes of a fixed basket of goods and services typically purchased by households.

concepts.name: GDP Deflator
concepts.definition: A measure of inflation reflecting the price changes of all goods and services produced domestically, using a changing basket that adjusts for consumption patterns.

concepts.name: Household Budget Impact
concepts.definition: The effect of inflation on household expenses, real income, savings, and borrowing costs.

concepts.name: Policy Communication
concepts.definition: The communication by central banks and governments about inflation to manage expectations and guide economic decisions.

processes.name: Measuring Inflation with CPI
steps: Select a fixed basket of consumer goods and services.
Track price changes of items in the basket over time.
Calculate weighted average price changes to determine CPI inflation rate.

processes.name: Measuring Inflation with GDP Deflator
steps: Consider all domestically produced goods and services.
Adjust basket composition to reflect current production and consumption patterns.
Calculate price changes to determine GDP deflator inflation rate.

processes.name: Policy Communication to Manage Inflation Expectations
steps: Central bank or government communicates inflation outlook and policy plans.
Households and businesses adjust spending, borrowing, and investment decisions accordingly.
Economic activity responds, influencing inflation dynamics.

misconceptions.name: CPI Basket Fixed vs GDP Deflator Variable Basket
misconceptions.description: A common confusion is that both CPI and GDP deflator measure inflation in the same way; however, CPI uses a fixed basket of goods and services, while GDP deflator uses a variable basket that changes with consumption and production patterns.

misconceptions.name: Scope of Inflation Measurement
misconceptions.description: Misunderstanding that CPI and GDP deflator cover the same goods and services; CPI focuses on consumer goods and services, whereas GDP deflator includes all domestically produced goods and services including investment and government spending.
